topic: Sharp optimal-transport / linear-fractional partial identification of the finite cutoff-shift, omega-weighted latent-rank Policy-Relevant Treatment Effect in a fuzzy regression-discontinuity design: use the observed status-quo treated branch as constraints and honest control-side extrapolation for the counterfactual branch; the identified set is the value of a linear-fractional program whose inner coupling reduces to product-cost 1D optimal transport; deliver the estimation rung (plug-in over the identified set at the honest boundary-nonparametric rate with Imbens-Manski x Armstrong-Kolesar coverage). Extends Tan-Blanchet-Syrgkanis (arXiv 2604.12263, OT partial-ID of PRTEs with IV) to the fuzzy-RD regime where within-x propensity overlap fails.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp identified interval for a finite cutoff shift's omega-weighted latent-rank PRTE in fuzzy RD when the observed status-quo branch is supplemented only by a continuous-covariate honest extrapolation class for the unobserved counterfactual branch.

source_refs.claim: PRTEs under limited propensity support are formulated as conditional OT and reduced to one-dimensional product-cost OT bounds; the paper does not cover fuzzy-RD finite cutoff shifts.
source_refs.author: Jiyuan Tan, Jose Blanchet, Vasilis Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: Fuzzy RD identifies a marginal threshold treatment effect under local policy invariance, not a finite cutoff-shift policy effect.
source_refs.author: Yingying Dong, Arthur Lewbel

source_refs.claim: Sharp policy-relevant bounds retain uncertainty about counterfactual treatment composition even when relevant marginals are known.
source_refs.author: Philip Marx

source_refs.excerpt: continuous covariates would require an additional measurability and regularization layer
the fuzzy-RD Wald denominator remains coupled

open_problems.why_unsolved: Tan--Blanchet--Syrgkanis address limited support in a generalized-IV model, whereas Dong--Lewbel's fuzzy-RD result is local to an infinitesimal threshold change. A finite move requires a continuation of the missing branch and a latent-rank mover composition; Marx shows why marginals alone do not settle that composition. The prior RD ledger independently identifies the missing measurable/regularized continuum layer and says the fuzzy denominator prevents importing a sharp-RD flow reduction. Thus neither the OT sharpness proof nor the fuzzy-RD extrapolation restriction presently supplies their joint feasible-set characterization.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharpness theorem equating the finite-shift PRTE identified set to extrema of a measurable linear-fractional program over branch laws and latent-rank couplings, with each endpoint reduced to product-cost one-dimensional OT and attained by a compatible construction.

writing_exemplar.why: Its arc moves from support failure in an observed assignment mechanism to a sharp distribution-aware OT identified set.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: policy changes require PRTEs -> limited assignment variation leaves relevant latent types unobserved -> a finite fuzzy-RD cutoff shift leaves a counterfactual branch and mover composition outside overlap

method_exemplars.technique: Formulate the target as constrained conditional OT and prove sharpness by reducing the coupling problem to separable one-dimensional product-cost OT.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Both targets optimize a policy functional over latent potential-outcome couplings constrained by observed branch laws.

method_exemplars.technique: Turn ratio endpoint search into signed fixed-multiplier value problems and certify the unique zero of the transformed objective.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The endpoint remains linear-fractional after the continuum coupling is introduced.

method_exemplars.technique: Use worst-case local-polynomial bias over a convex smoothness class and honest critical values for boundary functionals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The unknown control-side continuation is a boundary functional restricted by an honest extrapolation class.

open_problems.open_problem: Obtain honest boundary-uniform confidence sets for causal linear-fractional OT endpoints in fuzzy RD when active couplings or selectors tie, the optimum lies on a Fréchet/transport boundary, and the first-stage jump may be weak.

source_refs.claim: Bias-aware Anderson--Rubin-type confidence sets are valid for a point-valued fuzzy-RD parameter with weak identification and noncanonical RD designs.
source_refs.author: Claudia Noack, Christoph Rothe

source_refs.claim: Interval-identified scalar parameters require confidence intervals uniform in identification-region width.
source_refs.author: Guido Imbens, Charles Manski

source_refs.claim: OT set inference requires a value CLT and bootstrap treatment of directionally differentiable functionals, but not fuzzy-RD boundary bias or a weak first stage.
source_refs.author: Grigory Franguridi, Laura Liu

source_refs.excerpt: computing H_sigma, the Dinkelbach root, and a strict gap from the pointwise Frechet plug-in
The regular inference result is conditional on a novel smooth-root interior-curvature submodel

open_problems.why_unsolved: Noack--Rothe solve weak-jump and smoothing-bias problems for a point-valued local Wald target, while Imbens--Manski handles interval uncertainty after regular endpoint estimators exist. OT endpoint maps can instead be only directionally differentiable when optimizers switch, precisely the case the prior reviewer excludes by conditioning on a smooth-root interior submodel; its requested strict-gap/Dinkelbach analysis is not a coverage theorem. Franguridi--Liu supplies a non-RD OT component, but the simultaneous honest-boundary, weak-first-stage, and set-endpoint regime remains untreated.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A locally uniform directional expansion and coverage theorem for plug-in lower and upper OT/LFP endpoints, with a bias-aware weak-jump test inversion and an Imbens--Manski-style critical value valid across active-set switching.

writing_exemplar.why: It makes concrete failures of conventional fuzzy-RD intervals the main motivation for a validity theorem.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: fuzzy RD is a ratio of local discontinuities -> weak jumps and boundary bias defeat a delta-method interval -> partial-ID OT endpoints additionally introduce optimizer-switching and interval uncertainty

method_exemplars.technique: Invert bias-aware Anderson--Rubin-type local-linear tests so inference does not divide by an estimated weak jump.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The finite-shift endpoint inherits a fuzzy-RD denominator and must remain valid when its jump is small.

method_exemplars.technique: Use directional differentiability and a bootstrap designed for nonsmooth value maps rather than a regular influence-function expansion.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Tied active couplings make the fractional endpoint map directionally rather than ordinarily differentiable.

method_exemplars.technique: Control worst-case boundary bias over a smoothness class with honest critical values.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The counterfactual branch is estimated at a nonparametric boundary under exactly such a class.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the sharp identified interval for a fuzzy-RD local Wald effect when one-sided manipulation is bounded by a density envelope and covariate-cell caps rather than by an exact manipulation share.

source_refs.excerpt: the sharp identified set for the nonmanipulator local Wald parameter is exactly
replace it with a fuzzy exact-share reduction that matches the GRR fuzzy optimization

open_problems.why_unsolved: The reviewer points to Gérard--Rokkanen--Rothe's fuzzy optimization as the relevant benchmark, but the banked model retains covariate-law equality and external cell caps. Those restrictions make the latent retained-share allocation nonrectangular; the sharp-RD Lee reduction therefore cannot carry over because the fuzzy Wald denominator changes with the retained treatment margin.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharpness theorem equating the fuzzy-RD Wald identified set to extrema of a linear-fractional retained-law program, plus a primal data-generating construction attaining every endpoint.

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivation runs from manipulation invalidating RD continuity to sharp partial identification under one-sided restrictions.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: cutoff manipulation breaks classical RD continuity -> one-sided restrictions retain partial information -> what is the sharp fuzzy-Wald set when shares obey an envelope and retained-law constraints?

method_exemplars.technique: Convert manipulation restrictions into feasible latent retained distributions and establish sharpness with endpoint-law constructions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target is a manipulated fuzzy-RD Wald ratio with an enlarged, nonrectangular share geometry.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct confidence sets from the joint limiting behavior of estimated lower and upper endpoints.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The causal target is an interval once the fractional extrema are estimated.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive sharp bounds for a finite-cutoff-shift omega-weighted latent-rank PRTE in fuzzy RD under local rank similarity when the policy-induced composition of movers is unknown.

source_refs.claim: Local rank similarity identifies whole-population potential-outcome CDFs and QTEs at the fuzzy-RD threshold, not a finite policy shift.
source_refs.author: Zequn Jin, Yu Zhang, Zhengyu Zhang, Yahong Zhou

source_refs.claim: Counterfactual intermediate-policy effects retain uncertainty about which compliers change treatment, with sharp bounds from extreme couplings.
source_refs.author: Philip Marx

source_refs.claim: CCOT gives sharp PRTE bounds under limited IV support, but not a fuzzy-RD rank-similarity finite-policy theorem.
source_refs.author: Jiyuan Tan, Jose Blanchet, Vasilis Syrgkanis

open_problems.why_unsolved: Jin et al. recover threshold distributions from local rank similarity, while Marx shows that a nonlocal policy change depends on the unobserved pairing of latent ranks to movers. Rank similarity at one cutoff does not determine the rank composition of persons induced to move by a finite cutoff shift. The required compatibility argument joining rank restrictions, fuzzy-RD selection, and omega weighting has not been supplied by either literature.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp lower-and-upper-bound theorem for the weighted latent-rank PRTE with necessary-and-sufficient feasible couplings under local rank similarity and explicit extremal couplings attaining both endpoints.

writing_exemplar.why: It cleanly motivates local rank similarity as the extra information needed beyond standard fuzzy RD.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: fuzzy RD identifies local complier effects -> rank similarity recovers threshold distributions -> which latent ranks move under a finite cutoff change remains unknown

method_exemplars.technique: Translate local rank similarity into fuzzy-RD distributional restrictions for potential-outcome CDF functionals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Those restrictions provide the status-quo marginal constraints before optimizing the weighted policy functional.

method_exemplars.technique: Use countermonotone extremal treatment-effect distributions and rearrangement arguments for weighted MTE functionals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired PRTE is a weighted functional whose extrema depend on the same latent-rank pairing issue.

method_exemplars.technique: Use local distribution regression, functional CLTs, and multiplier bootstrap for fuzzy-RD distributional inputs.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Plug-in endpoints must estimate the rank-restricted marginal laws on which the extremal coupling is based.

open_problems.open_problem: Give necessary-and-sufficient testable feasibility conditions for a finite-shift fuzzy-RD OT identified set that jointly imposes local monotonicity, rank similarity, and an honest extrapolation restriction.

source_refs.claim: Fuzzy-RD LATE validity has observed-data inequality implications and a size-controlled specification test.
source_refs.author: Yu-Chin Hsu, Ji-Liang Shiu, Yuanyuan Wan

source_refs.claim: Local rank similarity in fuzzy RD produces moment restrictions and a test, but only for the threshold model.
source_refs.author: Zequn Jin, Yu Zhang, Zhengyu Zhang, Yahong Zhou

source_refs.claim: PRTE partial identification can be represented as constrained conditional-OT feasibility and optimization.
source_refs.author: Jiyuan Tan, Jose Blanchet, Vasilis Syrgkanis

open_problems.why_unsolved: Hsu--Shiu--Wan test conventional local monotonicity/continuity restrictions and Jin et al. test threshold rank similarity. Neither characterizes joint compatibility with a nonlocal continuation class and a common policy coupling. An OT feasible set can be empty despite nonrejection of every component restriction, so separate tests cannot validate the finite-shift identified set.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient observed-data or moment-inequality characterization of nonempty feasible couplings and a bootstrap-valid test of joint feasibility for the finite-shift fuzzy-RD OT model.

writing_exemplar.why: Its narrative treats falsification of fuzzy-RD identifying assumptions as part of the causal analysis.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: fuzzy-RD interpretation relies on local validity restrictions -> those restrictions have observed-data implications -> finite policy bounds additionally require joint OT and extrapolation feasibility

method_exemplars.technique: Express partial identification as constrained conditional OT and certify sharpness with primal feasible coupling constructions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The model is valid exactly when the augmented OT feasible set is nonempty.

method_exemplars.technique: Translate fuzzy-RD validity into estimated inequality restrictions and use a weighted-bootstrap specification test.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A projected feasibility characterization would be tested by the same inequality-testing architecture.

literature_map:
1. Tan, Blanchet, and Syrgkanis (2026, arXiv:2604.12263) frame limited propensity support as a PRTE identification failure and use conditional OT with one-dimensional product-cost reductions.
2. Marx (2024, Journal of Econometrics, 10.1016/j.jeconom.2023.105561) gives sharp policy bounds from marginal distributions and stochastic-order rearrangements.
3. Marx's policy-composition problem is directly relevant: a counterfactual policy leaves open which latent types move.
4. Dong and Lewbel (2015, Review of Economics and Statistics, 10.1162/rest_a_00510) identify a marginal threshold effect in sharp and fuzzy RD, exposing the finite-shift extrapolation gap.
5. Jin, Zhang, Zhang, and Zhou (2025, Econometric Theory, 10.1017/S026646662300021X) use local rank similarity to identify fuzzy-RD threshold distributions and QTEs.
6. Those rank restrictions do not determine the composition of movers under a nonzero cutoff change.
7. Hsu, Shiu, and Wan (2024, Journal of Econometrics, 10.1016/j.jeconom.2024.105738) supply observable implications and specification testing for fuzzy-RD LATE validity.
8. Noack and Rothe (2024, Econometrica, 10.3982/ECTA19466) give bias-aware AR-type fuzzy-RD inference robust to weak identification.
9. Imbens and Manski (2004, Econometrica, 10.1111/j.1468-0262.2004.00555.x) require interval-ID coverage to be uniform in set width.
10. Armstrong and Kolesar (2020, Quantitative Economics, 10.3982/QE1199) provide honest boundary inference over smoothness classes.
11. Franguridi and Liu (2025, arXiv:2512.18084) develop OT set inference for nonregular values, but not fuzzy-RD boundary/weak-jump inference.
12. The live intersection is finite fuzzy-RD extrapolation, latent-rank OT sharpness, and uniformly honest endpoint inference.
prior_proposal_map:
- Mined the adjacent partial-ID/RD candidate pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1, its proposal, README/state material, and all angle0 reviewer JSONs.
- Its scope flags retain sharp global-plus-cell trimming and affine-score fractional endpoint geometry; reviewer strengthening paths require a correct fuzzy-Wald/nonrectangular feasible-set treatment.
- Mined rejected angles from failed pid_rdd_flow_manipulation_v1; they explicitly leave the coupled fuzzy-Wald denominator outside their sharp-RD flow representation.
- Mined failed pid_alwaystaker_adrf_sharp_conditional_frechet_fractional_program reviewer JSONs; they exclude coverage at selector ties and Frechet-boundary optima.
- Mined failed pid_ot_dte_marginal_v1, which separately records the simultaneous-coupling obstacle in transport partial identification.
- No prior proposal exists under the current qid beyond the state/pipeline files.
